name: ID.me Identity Verification & Affiliation API
what_it_does: ID.me REST API for identity verification, group affiliation verification, and trusted digital identity network. Enables AI agents to manage identity proofing and verification for government benefits and restricted access programs, handle military, veteran, first responder, and student affiliation verification for discount and access programs, access healthcare worker identity verification for medical licensing and prescribing access, retrieve government employee identity verification for agency access control, handle NIST 800-63-3 identity assurance level (IAL) verification for high-assurance use cases, access biometric-optional identity verification via AI-powered document + selfie matching, retrieve trusted login and single sign-on data for identity federation, manage fraud prevention signals for account takeover detection, handle senior citizen and teacher group verification for benefit programs, and integrate verified identity data with government, healthcare, financial services, and retail platforms.
use_cases: Verifying government benefits eligibility from identity assurance agents
Authenticating veteran status from military discount agents
Verifying healthcare provider identity from medical access agents
Confirming group affiliations from benefit program agents
not_for: General KYC without government identity and group affiliation focus
Financial services AML/KYC without government benefit program context
International identity verification without US government network
best_when: A government agency, healthcare organization, retailer, or financial institution using ID.me wants AI agents to automate identity proofing, veteran and military affiliation verification, healthcare worker credentialing, government access control, and group discount eligibility verification.
avoid_when: PRIVACY RISK: ID.me processes biometric facial recognition data and government IDs — automated identity verification using facial recognition without informed consent and biometric data handling disclosure violates BIPA (Illinois), CIPA (Texas), and other state biometric privacy laws. Government benefit eligibility automation based on identity verification must maintain appeals and due process rights. ID.me identity data cannot be used beyond the specific verified purpose; secondary use restrictions apply.

auth.notes: ID.me uses OAuth 2.0 (OpenID Connect). Developer portal at developers.id.me. McLean, Virginia HQ. Private (VC-backed, $205M raised, Vista Equity Partners lead). Used by 35+ US states and federal agencies (IRS, VA, SSA) for identity verification. NIST SP 800-63 IAL2 and IAL3 certified. Military, veteran, first responder, healthcare, government employee, and student affiliation verification. Webhooks for verification events. Competes with Login.gov and Clear for government identity.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-verification or integration pricing
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-verification pricing for affiliation checks; enterprise contracts for government
pricing.notes: McLean, Virginia. Founded 2010. Private ($205M raised, Vista Equity Partners lead). 90M+ verified identities. 35+ US states use ID.me. Federal agencies: IRS (returns), VA (benefits), SSA. Military and veteran affiliation network. Healthcare worker verification. NIST 800-63 IAL2/3 certified identity proofing. Competes with Login.gov (federal), Socure, and Persona for digital identity.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; volume plan dependent

known_agent_gotchas: BIOMETRIC PRIVACY RISK: ID.me facial recognition verification triggers BIPA (Illinois), CIPA (Texas), and Washington state biometric privacy laws; automated biometric identity verification requires prior written consent and biometric data policy disclosure

Government benefit due process — government agency use of ID.me for benefit access decisions must preserve appeal and due process rights; automated eligibility denial without human review option creates constitutional due process issues
Scope restriction enforcement — ID.me verified data is scoped to specific purpose; automated reuse of verified identity data for unauthorized purposes violates data use agreement
IRS controversy — ID.me IRS partnership generated public controversy (2022); verify current IRS and agency relationship status for government integration
NIST IAL assurance levels — ID.me offers IAL1, IAL2, and IAL3; automated access decisions must use correct IAL level for the assurance requirement of the application

Affiliation verification shelf life — military/veteran status verification has a shelf-life; automated benefit decisions should re-verify at appropriate intervals rather than relying on stale verification
agent_readiness.security_notes: Government identity verification. SOC2, FedRAMP, NIST 800-63. OAuth2/OIDC. US. Government-verified identity and biometric data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Government identity verification. SOC2, FedRAMP. OAuth2/OIDC. US. Government-verified identity data.
